But it might not though. It could take weeks or months if those events don’t materialize. But it could be days if those specific volatility events do materialize.

Doesn’t seem constructive, worth saying. It’s litterally saying “the market can go up or sideways until it makes a lower low over the next days, weeks, or months. Okay… AND??? 

If we rally up to 5900 over 3 weeks… well, she did say “bull traps are a setup”… just took longer to setup and it made higher lower highs.

If chop for a month, same. If we crash in 2 days, same. If we rally up to 5850 in 10 days, same. Literally the market could trade anywhere between 4900-5900 with that statement over the next 2 months and be covered.

comment.body: it's all about probability, starting from a very simple case that assuming SPX is just a simple random walk (i.e. up/down are both 50%) now, given that we are at 5600, the probability we reach 5900 before 4900 is ??? then you can make it complicate, SPX is not a simple random walk, it has some shifts towards uptrend/downtrend, then what is that prob?? Alma is a quant, analyzing the market and giving us the risk levels as well as the conditions to reach those levels, she is not the god to give you a 100% sure claims, actually no one can, if you see someone saying that they can give you a 100% sure signals, then they are 100% talking bull shit...

comment.body:
Yes I am well away that everything from flying in a plane, eating from a seedy Chinese restaurant, or trading the markets is a probability distribution. Everything in life is. That said, because a plane could crash, does that mean I should refuse to do fly? No.

So in a service where we are looking to gain an edge by increasing our understanding of a probability distribution, how is it helpful or insightful to say “the market could go up or sideways over the next 2 days to 3 months as we set up a “bull trap”? Especially when the commentary of that probability distribution doesn’t look further out than 2-3 months? It’s actually like saying “the market could go sideways, raging up by 200-300 points, or crash 200-500 points over the next 2 months.”

I don’t know why this is hard for everyone to comprehend here. It’s like this thread has an autoimmune response to false pathogens.

comment.body:
Respectfully, I think you're focused on one part (the PA bull trap), then asking and looking only for specifics about that one part, not the entirety of what is unfolding. I get it though. The exact area of PA would be nice to know when to short or go long, but we all know nobody knows how the market will unfold day by day. 

Alma has been giving the macro context (as well as the quant details), geo and market dynamics, to show us how the market is currently mispricing a risk event. And if you recall in her mean reversion post, she refers to periods of time where that mispricing leads to corrections. The momentum pendulum swings and the psychology of that moment (which is apart of a trap, bear or bull) creates a volatility event. And so at the current time with less risk being priced in, if that momentum builds quickly, and the PA floats upward, thats when the swift move down can happen. 

That's how I am interpreting the idea and point of a bull trap.

comment.body:
My 2 cents. The consistent thing that you can be sure is actionable that you will get from this service are the intraday levels and the dealer flow analysis.

Anything beyond a day but probably a good read on the week are her weekly pivots and then goes to longer positioning like JPM collar or iron condor man. 

And then outside of this - what you’re getting is interpreting of the fed actions, the geo politician actions, and possibly quarter end options expiry/vixperation analysis. 

It’s like the layers to the onion. There are some core things that are very much actionable. But the speculative outer layers you have to understand are stabs at predictions the future that Alma is making her best interpretation of 
for our benefit. I’d rather have those interpretations than not have them because some educated guess is better than a zero guess from me

comment.body: A comment from the sidelines on potential timing.  As I understand Alma's weekly post (and not trying to put any words in her mouth), she suggests that because in relative terms skew is not bid, the market is underpricing a potential [insert your favorite word -- I'll use sh!tstorm] from the impending tariff issues, both whatever Trump does and however the EU might respond, with an emphasis on under appreciation of how strong the EU's response may be.  In making this observation, she looks at the skew for next week, April 15, and April OpEx.  My inference therefore is that the timeline for such an event -- and the timing of the bull trap setup -- is within 1-3 weeks, not a setup that might take months and months to play out.  This fits in with her broader view of timing that she's referenced repeatedly of a bottoming [note the use of the gerund, not a "bottom" but a "bottoming," i.e., a process] after Easter and into June.  And accepting that the goal of this bottoming is to provide a stable foundation for growth, it seems unlikely to be V-bottom, followed by lockout rally (think Nov 2023).  Putting this together in my mind, a potential downside surprise over the coming weeks precipitating a big drop, which then takes all wind out of the market's sails, the market then churns and grinds lower in a bottoming process through May into June as the geopolitical events Alma has described unfold and potentially resolve.

comment.body: It’s all good. Your take was definitely reasonable and how I view this “service” as well. Although, a lot of the layers of that onion can get conflated and timeframes can get mashed up very easily on intraday posts and the weekly posts. Sometimes it is tough to separate the wheat from the chaff… especially when commentary like the “bull trap” reference gets inserted into the mix, which ends up covering a very wide spread.

comment.body:
The takeaway I really have from her weekly post almost goes to the psychology of the moment we are in

Let’s say the market rips this week on relief that the tariffs are finally known and we are past that. The investor psychology could be a collective relief. Which also mirrors the complacency in the hedging she described 

Imagine we are there in that moment. It could be Thursday. Then NFP comes Friday and miraculously the jobless figures aren’t horrendous. We could get an even bigger
Rip Friday. The weekend arrives. Everyone is on a high. And then European countries or China on Sunday announce plans to stimulate their economies by divesting us bond holdings. That would be the bull trap

comment.body: Regarding bull traps,Here is my view of recent giant bull trap.Lots of people got trapped into Trump victory, and Golden Age promises, all dreams come true under Trump. Also notable how market rallied to retes that range and rejected last week. Many laggards that missed Biden's 2y long pump bought the Trump's top/trap,now holding bag. Thats a bull trap, also u can find small bull/bear traps on any time frame, depending on your imagination and interpretation. 👇
